data.title: Rithm Capital Faces Elevated Leasing Risk as Paramount Vacancies and 2026 Lease Expirations Threaten Cash Flows
data.description:
Rithm Capital Corp. (RITM) has disclosed a new risk, in the Costs category.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Rithm Capital Corp. faces meaningful leasing risk at its Paramount Assets, which had a 14.1% vacancy rate at its share as of December 31, 2025. With 537,500 square feet, or roughly 8.6% of annualized rents, scheduled to expire in 2026, the company may struggle to renew tenants or re-lease space at comparable or higher rental rates.

If rental rates decline, or if a significant portion of the expiring and currently vacant space is not promptly re-leased, the value of the Paramount Assets could be negatively impacted. Such outcomes would likely pressure Rithm’s business performance, results of operations, overall financial condition and future cash flows.

The average RITM stock price target is $14.50, implying 37.05% upside potential.

To learn more about Rithm Capital Corp.’s risk factors, click here.

data.title: Chimera Investment’s Non-QM Strategy Faces Rising Regulatory, Compliance and Litigation Risks
data.description:
Chimera Investment (CIM) has disclosed a new risk, in the Regulation category.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Chimera Investment operates in a highly regulated mortgage environment where its HomeXpress origination platform is subject to overlapping and evolving federal and state consumer lending and privacy laws, creating significant compliance and licensing risk across 46 states and the District of Columbia. The expanding scope of statutes like the CCPA/CPRA and data protection rules increases the burden of monitoring, audits and system changes, while ambiguous and inconsistent interpretations heighten the risk of violations, sanctions and operational constraints.

The company’s focus on Non-QM loans, which feature more flexible underwriting standards than qualified mortgages, exposes it to elevated credit, legal and litigation risk, particularly under economic stress when delinquencies and defaults may rise. Because Non-QM loans lack safe-harbor protections and often rely on subjective regulatory standards, adverse performance or challenges to their compliance could lead to higher loss severities, repurchase obligations and diminished investor appetite.

Chimera Investment is further exposed to federal and state anti-predatory lending regimes such as HOEPA and analogous state laws that impose complex disclosure, “net tangible benefit” and high-cost loan criteria, which are often subjective and open to reinterpretation. Any finding that loans it originates or acquires violate these rules could result in borrower rescission rights, monetary penalties and class action lawsuits, as well as reputational damage that may constrain future origination volumes and pricing power.

Given the broad and intensifying oversight of agencies such as the CFPB, DOJ and state regulators, together with shifting enforcement priorities and disparate-impact theories under fair lending statutes, Chimera Investment faces ongoing uncertainty around what constitutes compliant conduct. Even with existing compliance systems, it remains vulnerable to costly investigations, mandated business changes, fines and potential loss of licenses, any of which could materially and adversely affect its business, financial condition and results of operations.

The average CIM stock price target is $14.50, implying 6.70% upside potential.

To learn more about Chimera Investment’s risk factors, click here.

Quidelortho Corporation faces a material risk if it fails to establish and sustain robust internal controls over financial reporting and fraud prevention. President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Director Mike Rosinus has sold 11,602 shares of Avidbank Holdings stock in a transaction valued at $350,612, signaling a notable insider move at the company.

Recent Updates on AVBH stock

Avidbank Holdings’ shares reacted to its Q4 call, where management detailed a rebound from last quarter’s securities‑related loss, underpinned by strong loan and deposit growth, an expanding net interest margin, and improved efficiency. Analysts’ stance and targets are being driven mainly by this evidence of durable core earnings and better funding mix, offset by rising nonperforming assets, higher provisions and expenses, and sensitivity to deposit costs and rate-driven margin pressure.

The IPO-funded balance sheet cleanup and shift into higher-yielding securities support a more positive medium-term margin and growth profile, tempering concerns about recent credit volatility and a temporarily elevated tax rate. Overall, the mix of robust underlying profitability indicators with manageable but rising credit and cost headwinds explains why recent price targets and ratings are framed around normalized earnings power rather than the headline 2025 net loss.

Spark’s Take on AVBH Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, AVBH is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by financial statement quality concerns—2025 showed a major disconnect between positive net income and deteriorating operating results alongside a reported drop to $0 operating/free cash flow. Offsetting this, the earnings call points to strong operating momentum and guidance (double-digit growth, improved NIM/efficiency), and technicals show an uptrend, though overbought signals and an unhelpful negative P/E limit upside to the overall rating.
